subject: Geology
title: Charles Lyell
description: A fast breakdown of Charles Lyell for builders.

hook.text: Hutton had the idea. Lyell wrote the book that made the world believe it. Every geologist read his work.

buildup.text: Charles Lyell's Principles of Geology, published in 1830, popularized uniformitarianism for a wide audience.

discovery.text: Lyell showed with meticulous evidence that slow, observable processes could explain all geologic features.

twist.text: Darwin took Lyell's book on the Beagle. Deep time gave Darwin the vast timeline evolution needed to work.

climax.text: Lyell turned Hutton's radical idea into the working framework of geology. He made deep time mainstream.

punchline.text: The right book at the right time changes science.

quiz.question: What did Lyell's Principles of Geology popularize?
